items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again...
Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. The mock-heroic register of “Vague doubts haunt me once again...” is preserved, and the clipped parallelism “Shpak's…, the ambassador's…” keeps the comedic detective-like linkage without over-explaining. No issues.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. The repetitive, banal politeness and era-clash humor are fully retained; the stilted self-introduction reads intentionally awkward in English in the same way it does in Russian. No issues.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. The sudden authoritarian register and comic overreach are preserved cleanly and idiomatically. No issues.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. The archaic, melodramatic tone is maintained, and the blunt “old woman” keeps the comic insult/epoch gap. No issues.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. The subject assignment (“the demons” as agents) and the panicked, superstitious framing are preserved; the repetition keeps the comic rhythm. No issues.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. Elevated, quasi-biblical diction matches the Tsar’s worldview and preserves the atheist-era irony. No issues.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It captures the MC-like command and the sudden modern-party vibe without sounding like a literal “Everyone dance!” No issues.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. The breezy self-satisfaction and criminal opportunism are conveyed with a natural English idiom. No issues.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. Crucially avoids calling it “eggplant caviar,” preventing confusion while preserving the punchline inversion (“overseas” + humble eggplant). No issues.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. The comedic inventory cadence, the self-important “domestic,” and the trailing correction “Three” are preserved. No issues.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. The calm, sing-song fatalism and shared-madness joke are retained with the same repetitive structure. No issues.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
